# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "PeerAuthenticationPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: "default-allowall"   # Name of the policy, it's a unique identifier for this rule in Istio control plane and can be used to selectively enable or disable rules at runtime. This is optional but recommended as enabling all traffic by default allows you more flexibility when configuring your service mesh later on
 namespace: "your-namespace"  # Namespace of the policy, it's a unique identifier for this rule in Istio control plane and can be used to selectively enable or disable rules at runtime. This is optional but recommended as enabling all traffic by default allows you more flexibility when configuring your service mesh later on
spec:  
 mtlsMode: PERMISSIVE  # Set the mode of MTLs, it'll allow both plaintext and encrypted traffics for backward compatibility in case there are no other rules defined. It can be either STRICT or PERMISSIVE (default is permissive) when you want to enable mTLS
